new_classes.label_en: Other Still Wine in Containers of More Than 2 Litres – Other (CN 22042990)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Stillwein in Behältnissen von mehr als 2 Litern – Sonstige (KN 22042990)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes, in containers holding more than 2 litres, that bears neither a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) nor a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) and is not a semi-sparkling wine; classified under CN subheading 2204 29 90. Classification takes into account total dry matter content as defined by Additional Note 4A to Chapter 22, including non-volatile substances such as sugar, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colourants, and salts.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the key discriminating criteria for CN 22042990: still wine (non-carbonated), in containers of more than 2 litres, bearing neither PDO nor PGI status, and not being semi-sparkling wine. The reference to Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22 — specifically the definition of total dry matter (Gesamttrockenstoff) and the enumeration of non-volatile substances (sugar, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colourants, salts) — constitutes a measurement/classification criterion that cannot be fully formalised as OWL property restrictions without introducing a numeric dry-matter threshold datatype property. That quantitative dry-matter criterion is not yet modelled and reduces the coverage score. The disjointness with PDOStillWineLargeContainer and PGIStillWineLargeContainer sibling classes is expressed via complement restrictions on hasGeographicOriginQuality.
